workflows.conflict_resolution: Do not create profile rows until source-row inventory is established. Resolve model disagreements by preserving HPO IDs and source labels exactly, leaving uncertain terms as CANDIDATE notes for human review.
workflows.pr_scope: One bootstrap PR after profile creation and candidate rows validate.
workflows.notes: Finnish is documented in docs/translations but has no babelon/hp-fi.babelon.tsv profile. Create profile only from a reviewed source-row inventory, then mark all LLM output as CANDIDATE.

workflows.conflict_resolution: Do not create profile rows until source-row inventory is established. Resolve model disagreements by preserving HPO IDs and source labels exactly, leaving uncertain terms as CANDIDATE notes for human review.
workflows.pr_scope: One bootstrap PR after profile creation and candidate rows validate.
workflows.notes: Thai is documented in docs/translations but has no babelon/hp-th.babelon.tsv profile. Create profile only from a reviewed source-row inventory, then mark all LLM output as CANDIDATE.

workflows.conflict_resolution: Treat empty Arabic profile as bootstrap, not gap-fill. Resolve right-to-left and terminology disagreements with model triangulation and retain all output as CANDIDATE.
workflows.pr_scope: One bootstrap PR after profile creation and candidate rows validate.
workflows.notes: Arabic profile files exist but contain headers only. Treat separately from normal gap-fill because there are no source rows to export yet.

workflows.conflict_resolution: Use existing Japanese Babelon terminology first. Keep obsolete source labels visible with the 旧： prefix. Apply only when importer row count matches and model checks report no failures.
workflows.notes: Japanese candidate profile is complete. Preserve any existing provenance columns and force LLM output to CANDIDATE.

workflows.conflict_resolution: Prefer established Turkish medical terminology already present in hp-tr.babelon.tsv. For model disagreements, keep the more literal HPO label candidate unless both review lanes flag a failure; record unresolved terms as CANDIDATE for human review.
workflows.pr_scope: Open or update upstream PR after Turkish reaches zero NOT_TRANSLATED rows.
workflows.notes: Turkish has a medium backlog. Use batches to keep review manageable.

workflows.conflict_resolution: Prefer established Dutch terminology already present in hp-nl.babelon.tsv. Split broad disagreement clusters into smaller batches and keep uncertain rows as CANDIDATE rather than OFFICIAL.
workflows.pr_scope: Open or update upstream PR after Dutch reaches zero NOT_TRANSLATED rows.
workflows.notes: Dutch has the largest backlog. Use smaller batches if reviewers need definition-heavy review.
